programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Hartlepool, the heli-medics treat a man who's been hit by a train. Cyclist Trevor suffers serious injuries after a collision. And in Cumbria, a biker needs emergency surgery.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode is about the importance of powerful and resilient bonds. Cyril, who's 91, has been in a car crash. Five-year-old Roman has a pencil stuck in his ear.

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped: The Snacks Aisle
programme.desc: How many mangoes are in a dried mango pack? The team also crack the secret of caramel popcorn and the mystery of snowballs' fluffy centre.

programme.title: Discover Must Have Ideas: Shopping
programme.desc: Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.

programme.title: Discover Must Have Ideas: Shopping
programme.desc: Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Herefordshire, Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo meet George, a collector for over 70 years. Max finds a crate of vintage motor oil bottles that could be worth a small fortune.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In the Devon countryside, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet collectors Derek and Daphne. Henry finds a very rare tractor, while Simon discovers a Raleigh Shopper bike.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie host at Watersedge Guest House in Barton on Sea. Mike and Danny waste no time in grumbling about the grime in their room. Can a spot of deer-watching win them over.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Tim and Amelia welcome their rivals to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast. But Sylvie and Alan aren't sure what 'boutique' means..

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Austrian-born artist Eva hosts in her cosmopolitan B&B, Roundhouse Hotel, on Weymouth seafront. Mike and Danny are shocked by the exposed shower.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Showmen Mike and Danny host at the four-star Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. Sylvie and Alan have a great view... of the local car wash. And at breakfast, Eva sends her food back twice.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day and the B&Bers meet one last time to find out how much they've been paid. Only one establishment can take the title of best value for money B&B. Who will it be.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fun-loving Jane serves up classic dishes with a twist and matches her wine to her food, on a night featuring burnt buns, jammy finds and marriage revelations.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Cassy hopes to gallop into first place with a jockey theme and gastropub grub. But how will her snail racing on the dinner table go down with her guests.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Veteran punk Cathy McConaghy goes back to her Irish roots, with homemade soda bread, cockle soup, oysters and a very boozy dessert that packs a punch.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Simon Meyer takes a laid-back approach to hosting and narrowly avoids a kitchen cat-astrophe. But he gets emotional over his chicken soup starter.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Local 'toff' Alasdair Moore plans to impress his guests with 'proper' dinner party food and fine wines. But an early slip-up dents his confidence.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Black Lion in Halland, near Lewes, host Nigel takes his guests to a salsa night, where he impresses with his 'snake hips'. But the lack of coffee refills at breakfast causes concern.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ken Clutterbuck welcomes his rivals to The Wheatsheaf in Exning, near Newmarket. Ken claims to be the host with the most, but his sense of humour doesn't go down well with guest Nigel.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sherrill Tacy hosts at the Sherwood Guesthouse in New Brighton on the Wirral, where some of her guests are stunned by the value for money, but Nigel goes looking for cleaning misdemeanours.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mark and Rachael host at the ski-themed Big Bear Lodge in Shropshire, where an afternoon of canoeing raises a few laughs, but accusations of cheating cause a rift.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for one last time to confront their critics and find out how much they've been paid and who's won.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil help first-time buyers in Manchester. Robyn and Alice want a classic Corrie redbrick. Steph and Christopher have been outbid 10 times in Didsbury and Chorlton.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Sophie and Paul are butting heads over their three-bed in Aylesbury. Can Kirstie's bold plan give them the space they need, or will Phil's alternatives win them over.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Armed with £100k and nothing but determination and forest timber, Marcus and Abi defy convention by building an off-grid home with no detailed plans in 20 acres of Pembrokeshire woodland.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Josh, who's 10, arrives at A&E after an accident at school, unaware that his finger was cut off in the incident. Doctors must work out how to reattach it, and how to tell him.

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman who's had a panic attack, a two-year-old with a high temperature, a woman who's collapsed, and a man who's recovering from knee surgery.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Yorkshire, Neil needs urgent attention after falling from his motorbike. And in the Lakes, medics scramble to the banks of Ullswater when a walker goes into cardiac arrest.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Josh, who's 10, arrives at A&E after an accident at school, unaware that his finger was cut off in the incident. Doctors must work out how to reattach it, and how to tell him.

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman who's had a panic attack, a two-year-old with a high temperature, a woman who's collapsed, and a man who's recovering from knee surgery.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: What's the secret to the bubbles in bubble tea? How come sour milk's bad but soured cream is great? And, from Portugal, the benefits of canned fish.

programme.title: Discover Must Have Ideas: Shopping
programme.desc: Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.

programme.title: Discover Must Have Ideas: Shopping
programme.desc: Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo meet retired haulier Jim in Worcestershire. Max wants to transform old air tanks into a sports car, while Simon turns some barrels into a bar bench.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry picks a pedal car and Simon grabs a yacht. Buying them is easy. Restoring them is not.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit is to the music-themed Harmony B&B in Torquay, where Dutch couple Hans MacDaniel and Monica van der Meij hope to wow their guests on the 'English Riviera'.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jenny and Richard Harmer welcome their rivals to the patriotically decorated Chimneys B&B in North Walsham. But is it disrespectful to use a flag as a tablecloth.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Bev and Caroline host at Wordsworth B&B in Cockermouth, Cumbria. At a spa visit, 'up for anything' Dutchman Hans MacDaniel is quick to volunteer for a spray tan.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Geordie mum and daughter Leslie and Emily Gray welcome their rivals to the Park Farm Guest House. But sparks fly over the north/south divide.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet one last time to settle some scores and find out how much they've been paid, before the winner is announced.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Live wire Ptol is convinced that high-quality food and fine wine will win him the prize. But his guests find his menu underwhelming and his hosting style over the top.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Francis Leigh serves a Thai meal with a twist, on a weird and wonderful night featuring dead flowers on the table and a song about spring rolls.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Patricia plans a Latin party with Peruvian food, on a night of singing and dancing. But Ptol mistakenly thinks he's eating llama instead of lamb..

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Care home worker Georgia Groves hosts a black-tie affair, complete with red carpet, posh frocks... and a spray tan booth.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent William Tilsley serves up a vegetarian menu and a blind-tasting food game. But one guest ends up spitting wine over another..

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at One 4 B&B in Cheltenham, where debonair host and part-time art dealer Nick Purchase proves his naked ambition with a life drawing class for his guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gloria Barnett and Dave Goulding host at the Windy Bottom B&B in Maidstone. But Nick's not dazzled by the bling in his Versace-themed boudoir and there are dirty tricks on a trip to a maze.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Phil and Angie Sutcliffe host at the Jolly Carter pub in Bolton. Despite Angie's nerves, the room inspections go well. But will their guests be as keen on their roller disco entertainment.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: John hopes that the scaffolding outside the Alexandra Hotel in Llandudno won't put off his guests. But there are slip-ups with towels and teabags, and Gloria gets locked in the loo.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for one last time to settle some scores and find out how much they've been paid - and who's won.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, Karen and Richard, who have a dream £1m budget, keep falling at the final hurdle, while Camilla and Liam want rid of renting so they can start a family.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Nikki and Bash's Birmingham three-bed is bursting at the seams. Can Kirstie sort out the dysfunctional layout, or will Phil's finds tick everything off the wish list.

programme.title: The Orient Express: A Golden Era of Travel
programme.desc: The Express heads for Munich via Germany's 'City of Gold' - Pforzheim - before stopping at Salzburg for an unexpected musical tribute.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Grant, who's 34, has been crushed against a brick wall by his own car while he was working on it. Doug, who's 44, is rushed to A&E with severe bleeding from his head.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Kayla, who's five, is rushed to St George's struggling to breathe. She has a rare form of juvenile arthritis and has spent months at Great Ormond Street under specialist care.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Cambridgeshire, the heli-medics race to treat a cyclist with a time-critical brain injury. On the Norfolk Broads, Tom breaks his thigh bone jumping off a boat.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Grant, who's 34, has been crushed against a brick wall by his own car while he was working on it. Doug, who's 44, is rushed to A&E with severe bleeding from his head.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Kayla, who's five, is rushed to St George's struggling to breathe. She has a rare form of juvenile arthritis and has spent months at Great Ormond Street under specialist care.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: What's the 'flower' in cauliflower wonders Kate? Plus the long life of lemon juice. And a fess-up at the brewery as the meaning of 'bottle conditioned' beer is revealed.
